task_completion.description: Open the 'Fly Brain' sample stack in ImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ interface showing an image window titled 'Fly Brain' (or similar) displaying a grayscale brain structure, with a slider at the bottom indicating it is a stack (57 slices).

task_completion.description: Open the tool to create a depth-coded projection (e.g., Temporal-Color Code).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for 'Temporal-Color Code' (accessed via Image > Hyperstacks) or a similar Z-coding tool is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Select the 'Fire' LUT or a similar spectrum LUT for the projection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LUT dropdown menu in the depth-coding dialog is set to 'Fire' or another spectrum color map.
A new image window displaying a colorful 2D projection of the fly brain. Different structures should appear in different colors from the spectrum LUT, indicating their varying Z-depths.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting RGB color image as 'depth_coded_fly_brain.png' to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the filename 'depth_coded_fly_brain.png' and the directory '~/ImageJ_Data/results/' (or '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/'), or a terminal/file explorer confirming the file has been saved in this location.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through ImageJ menus and dialogs rather than executing terminal commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a pre-written script or macro to automatically generate the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent manually configures the projection and saves the file, rather than running a script from the terminal or macro runner.

integrity.description: The resulting image is genuinely depth-coded.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final image shows a multi-colored projection where different depths appear in different colors. It should not look like a standard Max Intensity Projection with a single color map uniformly applied.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Fly Brain' sample is a built-in 256x256 pixel confocal stack with 57 slices. The expected output path is '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/depth_coded_fly_brain.png'. The depth coding is typically done via Image > Hyperstacks > Temporal-Color Code. Genuine depth coding shows a correlation between hue and Z-depth, resulting in a multi-colored projection where different depths appear in different colors from the chosen LUT (e.g., 'Fire').
